title: SDN Configuration Change and Rollback Audit
exclusions: Single-vendor Cisco NDFC intent-compliance drift grids confined to Nexus Dashboard Fabric Controller objects (see UC-18.4.2 — different drift semantics); Kubernetes CNI policy verdict churn (UC-18.3.1); SDN controller cluster quorum or dataplane tunnel overlay health alone without audit payloads (UC-18.3.3 / UC-18.3.4); passive NetFlow-only telemetry lacking controller-submitted transaction identifiers; offline archival backups that never emitted REST or syslog audit rows.
dataSources: `index=sdn_audit` / `index=network_operations` unions spanning **`sourcetype=opendaylight:karaf`** (**CONFIG_DATASTORE_WRITE**, **DATASTORE_DELETE**) plus **`opendaylight:jmx`** counters and **`karaf`** **`log4j2`** audit appenders; **`sourcetype=openstack:neutron:audit`** from Audit Middleware fan-out through RabbitMQ into **HEC** with Neutron REST **PUT**/**POST**/**DELETE** echoes; **`sourcetype=onos:audit`** capturing **`netcfg`** cluster-broadcast commits beside **`GET`** **`/onos/v1/cluster`** posture polls; **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit`** sourcing **`aaaModLR`** Managed Object writes via **`GET`** **`https://<apic>/api/node/class/aaaModLR.json`** plus syslog **`F0467`** AAA-mod traces; **`sourcetype=vmware:nsxt:audit`** mirroring **`AuditEntry`** bodies from **`GET`** **`https://<nsx-manager>/policy/api/v1/infra/realized-state`** complements plus **`/policy/api/v1/infra`** audits where deployed; **`sourcetype=ovs:ovsdb`** for **`ovsdb-server`** transactions paired with **`ovs-vsctl`** privilege markers; optional **`dna:center:audit`**, **`crosswork:controller:snapshot`**, **`apstra:audit`** feeds — all keyed by **`transaction_id`**, **`object_dn_or_uri`**, and **`controller_node`**.
app: **Splunk Connect for Syslog** ([documentation](https://splunk.github.io/splunk-connect-for-syslog/main/)), **Splunk HTTP Event Collector**, **`Splunk REST API Modular Input`** ([Splunkbase 1546](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)), **`Splunk Add-on for VMware ESXi`** contexts where NSX appliances forward syslog, optional **Cisco ACI** integrations landing **`cisco:aci:audit`** via programmatic collectors — compose a private **`props.conf`**/**`transforms.conf`** TA co-located on Search Heads.
spl:
(index=sdn_audit OR index=network_operations OR index=os)
(sourcetype="opendaylight:karaf" OR sourcetype="openstack:neutron:audit" OR sourcetype="onos:audit" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:audit" OR sourcetype="ovs:ovsdb")
earliest=-24h
| eval actor=trim(lower(coalesce(user, usr, audit_user, actor, initiated_by)))
| eval txn=trim(coalesce(transaction_id, txn_id, request_id, audit_transaction, cluster_txn))
| eval obj=trim(coalesce(object_dn_or_uri, mo_dn, resource_uri, netcfg_path))
| eval cls=trim(coalesce(object_class, mo_class, resource_type, cfg_section))
| eval act=trim(lower(coalesce(action_type, action, operation, evt_name)))
| eval ctrl=trim(coalesce(controller_node, apic_node, nsx_appliance, onos_instance, odl_shard))
| eval before_val=coalesce(before_value, old_json, prior_body)
| eval after_val=coalesce(after_value, new_json, realized_body)
| eval rollback_hint=case(match(lower(act),"rollback|undo|restore|reverse"),1,match(lower(coalesce(result,normalized)),"rollback_ok"),1,1=1,0)
| eval hour_bucket=strftime(_time,"%H")
| eval after_hours=if((hour_bucket>=22 OR hour_bucket<=5),1,0)
| eval biz_hours=if(after_hours=0,1,0)
| eval diff_snippet=if(len(after_val)>240,substr(after_val,1,240)." …",substr(coalesce(after_val,""),1,240))
| eventstats dc(obj) as txn_objects by txn
| eval blast_radius=coalesce(txn_objects,1)
| transaction txn maxpause=300ms maxspan=24h keeporphans=true fields actor act cls obj ctrl source_ip session_id
| eval paired_reverse=if(rollback_hint=1 AND isnotnull(txn),mvindex(act,-1),null())
| eval pair_window=if(isnotnull(txn) AND mvcount(act)>1,strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),null())
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen values(actor) as actors sum(after_hours) as after_hours_hits max(blast_radius) as blast_radius_latest values(ctrl) as controllers earliest(pair_window) as rollback_pair_hint values(before_val) as before_snap values(after_val) as after_snap values(diff_snippet) as diff_preview count as edits
    by txn sourcetype obj cls
| sort - blast_radius_latest - edits
| head 400
| eval actor=mvindex(actors,0)
| join type=left actor max=0
    [(index=auth sourcetype IN ("cisco:acs:radius","tacacs:session") earliest=-24h)
     | rename username as actor | stats latest(_time) as auth_ts by actor]
| eval auth_age_seconds=abs(last_seen-auth_ts)
| eval auth_seen=if(isnotnull(auth_ts) AND auth_age_seconds<=120,1,0)
| stats sum(edits) as total_edits dc(actor) as distinct_actors sum(after_hours_hits) as after_hours_volume max(blast_radius_latest) as max_objects_per_txn values(sourcetype) as fabrics dc(txn) as txn_count avg(auth_seen) as auth_alignment
    by actor
| sort - total_edits - max_objects_per_txn
| head 50
description: Correlates heterogeneous SDN controller audits: OpenDaylight **CONFIG_DATASTORE_WRITE**/**DATASTORE_DELETE** via syslog plus Karaf **log4j2** (optional **JMX**); Neutron **config-change** plus Audit Middleware CADF via RabbitMQ→**HEC** with REST **PUT**/**POST**/**DELETE** echoes; ONOS **netcfg** broadcasts beside **/onos/v1/cluster** polls; Cisco **aaaModLR** via **/api/node/class/aaaModLR.json** and syslog **F0467**; VMware NSX **AuditEntry** from Policy APIs (legacy **/api/v1/audits** where still exposed); **ovsdb-server**/**ovs-vsctl** traces; optional DNA Center / Crosswork / Apstra feeds plus **commit-confirmed** rollbacks. Splunk ties **TACACS+**/RADIUS, ServiceNow **CHG**, IaC hashes, Vault hints, blast radius per **transaction_id**, after-hours tagging, rollback pairing, and intent-vs-realized divergence so rogue edits rank ahead of benign automation bursts.
value: Unauthorized edits on heterogeneous SDN controllers bypass classical device-syslog correlation: one credential can fan policy across tenants before routers complain. Treating OpenDaylight, Neutron, ONOS, APIC, NSX-T, and OVS audit feeds as one timeline ties identities to edits, scopes blast radius per transaction, catches orphan rollbacks, and shortens Sev1 bridges.
implementation: Quick win: unify **`index=sdn_audit`** sourcetypes behind one dashboard tile showing top **`actor`** edits plus **`transaction`** blast radius before layering enrichment — proves ingest parity early. Normalize heterogeneous audit feeds with **`FIELDALIAS`** maps for **`user`**, **`transaction_id`**, **`object_dn_or_uri`**, **`before_value`**, **`after_value`**, **`controller_node`**; land AAA (**TACACS+**/RADIUS) beside **`auth`** indexes; ingest ServiceNow **`CHG`** extracts or MID-tier lookups; schedule hourly correlation searches plus **`transaction`** rollback pairing macros; publish dashboard tiles for top actors, blast radius, after-hours spikes, rollback twins—wire optional **Splunk Enterprise Security** adaptive responses keyed on **`blast_radius_latest`** thresholds.
visualization: (1) Horizontal bar — **total_edits** by **`actor`** (top changers last 24h). (2) Single-value tile — **`max_objects_per_txn`** blast radius threshold breach count. (3) Column chart — **`after_hours_volume`** stacked by **`hour_bucket`** / **`after_hours`** flag. (4) Timeline — **`rollback_pair_hint`** sequences grouped by **`txn`** showing **`before_snap`** vs **`after_snap`**. (5) Heat matrix — **`distinct_actors`** × **`fabrics`** (**sourcetype** facets) change velocity density. (6) Table drilldown — **`txn`**, **`actors`**, **`controllers`**, **`fabrics`**, **`blast_radius_latest`**, **`auth_seen`** with deep-link to raw events.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(_time) as lt values(Change.object_category) as oc values(Change.action) as ca count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes where nodename=Change.All_Changes by Change.user Change.src
| join Change.user max=0 [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(_time) as auth_t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication where nodename=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user Authentication.src]
| eval correlation_gap=if(isnull(auth_t) OR abs(lt-auth_t)>900,"possible_shadow_actor","linked")
| where correlation_gap="possible_shadow_actor"
| sort - lt
| head 200
schema: CIM: Change (object_category, action, command, object attrs, src/dvc fields) intersected with Authentication (user, src, app) when AAA flows normalize into Splunk indexes feeding accelerated models.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **Change** and **Authentication** once FIELDALIAS maps vendor columns (**aaaModLR**, **AuditEntry**, **CONFIG_DATASTORE_WRITE**) into **`command`**, **`object`**, **`user`**, **`src`** — retain parallel raw indexes because heterogeneous MO naming often breaks pure **`tstats`** coverage.

references.title: Cisco APIC REST API — aaaModLR managed object overview
VMware NSX-T Policy API — Audit query patterns
OpenDaylight Documentation — Controller runtime and Karaf logging
OpenStack Audit Middleware — CADF audit formats
MITRE ATT&CK — T1098 Account Manipulation & T1078.001 Valid Accounts

knownFalsePositives:
**CI/CD burst signatures** — Pipeline controllers replay manifests rapidly while transaction IDs refresh each push; hourly velocity resembles misuse though pipeline_job_id stamps map cleanly to Jenkins Git hashes—suppress against automation principal lists plus CMDB-owned deployment waves.

**Terraform plan-and-apply twins** — Two CONFIG_DATASTORE_WRITE waves appear minutes apart during speculative plan versus executed apply while txn_state briefly resembles rollback_candidate though desired_config parity holds—tie suppression to workspace run_phase extracted from Terraform stdout mirrored into auxiliary indexes.

**Consensus replay mirrors** — Fresh RAFT followers replay immutable ovsdb journals after quorum heal so AuditEntry duplicates inflate blast_radius counts until leader_epoch increments stabilize—dedupe txn plus controller_node pairs before paging.

**Warm-site journaling floods** — Secondary controllers replay aaaModLR backlog after DR failover while Vault leases rotate; after_hours detectors flash despite CAB-approved emergency tickets—gate alerts with CHG business_service qualifiers excluding DR drill markers plus intent-hash parity.

**Compliance scanner cadence** — Nightly Ansible dry-run triples Neutron PUT counts against golden policy mirrors without dataplane drift—whitelist svc-compliance actors inside openstack:neutron:audit before escalating DNA Center audit echoes timed with scanner windows.

premiumApps.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
premiumApps.note: optional, for risk-based alerting on correlated identities

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Heavy Forwarders terminating TLS syslog listeners plus dual-stack **HEC** nodes sized for burst **CONFIG_DATASTORE_WRITE** EPS from OpenDaylight controllers; RabbitMQ shovel lanes feeding **openstack:neutron:audit** JSON without starving sourcer queues.
• Dedicated **index=sdn_audit** with retention aligned to audit policy; Search Heads ship a private TA (**TA-sdn_audit_normalize**) bundling shared **FIELDALIAS**, **EVAL**, and **props.conf** tied to listed sourcetypes.
• Secrets for HTTPS collectors (**aaaModLR**, NSX Policy APIs, ONOS REST) sourced from Vault or CyberArk—never paste bearer tokens into **savedsearches.conf**.
• Roles granting **srchIndexesAllowed** for **sdn_audit**, **auth**, optional **servicenow_chg**, optional **vault_audit**.

Step 1 — Data sources & ingest paths
• OpenDaylight — **Splunk Connect for Syslog** ingests Karaf syslog carrying **CONFIG_DATASTORE_WRITE**/**DATASTORE_DELETE**; Universal Forwarder **monitor://** tails **log4j2** RollingFile paths under Karaf **data/log**; optional scripted **inputs** emit **opendaylight:jmx** counters from infrautils MBeans for datastore churn rates beside raw audit lines.
• OpenStack Neutron — Audit Middleware publishes CADF envelopes; shovel into **HEC** landing **openstack:neutron:audit**, preserving REST verbs aligned with neutron-server PUT/POST/DELETE audit trails for subnet/router/policy edits.
• ONOS — Combine Karaf audit tails with REST collectors harvesting **netcfg** cluster-broadcast apply narratives while polling **GET /onos/v1/cluster**/**devices** so controller_node stays attributable during partitioned edits.
• Cisco APIC — Modular REST pulls against **aaaModLR** JSON alongside syslog **F0467** AAA modifications forwarded via SC4S into **cisco:aci:audit**, stamping **apic_node** from reply headers for shard attribution.
• VMware NSX-T — Incremental pulls of Policy/realized audit payloads into **vmware:nsxt:audit** plus syslog bridges from NSX appliances over TLS—checkpoint cursor files prevent duplicate AuditEntry ingestion across poll intervals.
• Open vSwitch — **ovs:ovsdb** tags ovsdb-server operational logs plus sudo-wrapped **ovs-vsctl** audit trails mirrored through Linux **auditd** execve records when ovs-vsctl spans sudo boundaries.

Step 2 — Field extractions, props/transforms
• JSON feeds (**openstack:neutron:audit**, NSX HTTPS JSON): **KV_MODE=json** plus **spath** safeguards for unicode-heavy Neutron policy blobs; extract **user**, **action_type**, **object_class**, **object_dn_or_uri**, **before_value**, **after_value**, **controller_node**, **transaction_id**, **source_ip**, **session_id**, **change_window**, **txn_state**.
• **opendaylight:karaf**: **EXTRACT** patterns for transaction_id tokens following **CONFIG_DATASTORE_WRITE** banners; map LDAP or API usernames into **user** consistently with AAA streams.
• **cisco:aci:audit**: **FIELDALIAS** for dn→**object_dn_or_uri**, ind→**transaction_id** where available, descr fields into **after_value** snapshots of **aaaModLR** payloads.
• **transforms.conf** optional **FORMAT** clones to secondary **auth** index for AAA lines that embed the same session_id as SDN edits, aiding **Authentication** model alignment.

Step 3 — Initial validation
• Time-align **transaction_id** windows to **tacacs:session** or **cisco:acs:radius** success events inside a two-minute envelope; absent authentication preceding datastore writes triggers investigation queues referencing intent-vs-realized divergence dashboards (orphaned automation secrets versus interactive shells).
• Compare Splunk edit timestamps with ServiceNow **CHG** planned_start/planned_end states—implemented-but-unlogged emergencies still carry bridge markers even when SNOW automation lags.
• Git correlation: nightly export maps Terraform workspace commits and Ansible Tower job IDs into lookups (**git_commit**, **ansible_job**) stamped inside audit comments or pipeline labels—spot-check commits against vendor GUI timelines weekly.

Step 4 — SPL building blocks
• Macro **sdn_audit_base** wraps index unions so dashboards/alerts stay synchronized when governance splits prod/nonprod indexes.
• **eval** derives **hour_bucket** and **after_hours** flags; **eventstats dc(obj) as txn_objects by txn** feeds **blast_radius**; **transaction txn maxpause=300ms maxspan=24h keeporphans=true** sequences multi-step edits; **stats** aggregates after **transaction** collapse top changers per **txn** and **obj**.
• **join type=left** maps actors onto parallel **Authentication** searches indexed by normalized username—populate **auth_seen** booleans feeding ES risk modifiers when Splunk Enterprise Security is licensed.

Step 5 — Comprehensive enrichment patterns (deepest layer)
• Cross-index joins: schedule summary collections (**collect**) capturing hourly blast_radius percentiles from raw lanes without replaying entire indexes during tier-2 hunts.
• Identity enrichment: **lookup tacacs_username_map** aligns Authorization profiles with approved maintenance slices; IAM rotations flagged via **vault_audit** secret lease bursts clarify benign Terraform-driven rotations versus rogue credential persistence (**MITRE** **T1098**, **T1078.001** storyboarding inside ES notable templates).
• CMDB lookups (**lookup cmdb_ci**) enrich **controller_node** with owning squad mailboxes—Adaptive Response actions attach SOC queues automatically when blast_radius_latest crosses thresholds while Authentication correlation_gap stays elevated.
• ServiceNow automation: **lookup snow_chg** maps txn anchors embedded in audit notes (CHG tokens, REQ IDs) into workflow states—planned freeze windows suppress benign bursts triggered during CAB-approved terraform waves yet still retain forensic copies.
• IaC lineage: correlate Terraform Cloud run IDs or Atlantis unlock markers mirrored through auxiliary indexes—when desired_checksum from Terraform diverges from sha256(**after_value**) hashes pulled via **rex**, escalate intent-vs-realized divergence narratives distinct from noisy drift scanners (Crosswork versus APIC aaaModLR comparisons stay controller-plane scoped).
• Vault posture: append searches against **vault_audit** sourcetype explaining synchronous bursts when PKI backends rotate tokens coinciding with controller API retries—differentiates noisy automation from staged lateral movement pivoting SDN APIs after Vault misuse.
• Splunk Enterprise Security: leverage risk aggregation combining Authentication failure ratios with edit velocity sigma scores (**streamstats** window=120 over txn buckets); adaptive responses throttle duplicate notables via **outputlookup fabric_audit_exceptions** for grandfathered automation accounts awaiting recertification.
• CIM alignment: accelerate **Change**/**Authentication** once **FIELDALIAS** maps vendor fields into **command**, **object**, **user**, **src**—use **tstats summariesonly=t** sparingly until field coverage audits show >85% population; retain raw indexes for forensic diff review.
• OVS pairing: **multisearch** union [linux audit execve searches] with **ovs:ovsdb** entries when implicit transaction identifiers are missing, synthesizing surrogate keys only for investigation—never solely for punitive alerting.
• Privacy: **rex mode=sed** redacts password key material from **before_value**/**after_value** strings before exporting evidence packs; **eval** scrub functions remove tokens logged by mistake in API payloads.
• Persistence cadence: maintain CSV snapshots of suppressed automation principals refreshed nightly from IAM exports so dormant CI identities do not silently inherit stale suppression rows after workforce churn or PKI resets break unattended pipelines tied to ovsdb mirrors on Linux gateways.
• Capacity governance: sample `_internal` metrics weekly — sourcer queues backing RabbitMQ shovel feeds must stay below negotiated EPS envelopes per sourcetype even during terraform sprint windows when engineers intentionally replay stacks dozens of times before merges finalize.
• Evidence replay discipline: whenever auditors challenge an alert bundle, regenerate diff previews without exporting entire MO payloads — truncate structured snapshots inside Splunk transforms before emailing SOC leads so privileged tenant descriptors never leak via ticketing attachments routed outside regulated VLANs.
• Cross-team narration: capture lightweight Markdown run-notes beside each suppression macro explaining why Jenkins-backed bursts differ from interactive AAA sessions — future reviewers inherit rationale without deciphering stale Slack threads referencing retired Atlantis installs or obsolete PKI anchors tied to retired DNA Center pods still mirrored inside syslog relays during hybrid migrations between controllers.
• Sampling drills: quarterly rotate synthetic edits through lab controllers spanning OpenDaylight oxygen shards and NSX unified clusters — replay Splunk pipelines comparing `_time` skew versus `_indextime` to tune freshness alarms without leaning on synthetic syslog floods alone or duplicated RAFT snapshots mistaken for stealth edits during staged chaos drills.

Step 6 — Operational runbook
• CAB rhythm: weekly review Splunk velocity leaders vs. backlog—escalate tier-2 reviews when actor-level sigma exceeds agreed bounds or when multiple sourcetypes spike without shared CHG bridges.
• Emergency change: require dual approver acknowledgement in SNOW work_notes with Splunk txn anchors copied verbatim—links raw **transaction** spans to incident bridges for auditors.
• Rollback confirmation: mandate explicit rollback verbs or commit-confirmed completions within one hour for sev1-related transactions; missing rollback pairings after high blast_radius_latest fire pager callbacks with PRB templates tied to fabric segment owners.
• Quarterly audit-cycle SOP: rotate collector credentials, validate TLS pinning on APIC/NSX/ONOS endpoints, regression-test **FIELDALIAS** suites via pytest against sample fixtures checked into Git to avoid silent parser drift across ODL/NSX upgrades; cadence KPI expectations assume MTBF aligned with vendor-published controller uptime budgets while SOC-facing MTTR targets for contested rollback investigations stay inside typical CAB emergency envelopes unless telemetry proves systematic indexer backlog unrelated to controllers.
grandmaExplanation: We keep a shared diary of who touched the knobs on each vendor's programmable-network brain—and whether those edits matched agreed windows and matching logins. When someone pushes risky changes late at night or walks back what they did without saying so, we raise it plainly before trouble spreads.
hardware: Cisco APIC-M3 appliance clusters fronting ACI fabrics; VMware NSX Manager Unified appliances or clustered NSX Policy/VIP deployments (release trains aligned with supported Splunk TA guidance); OpenDaylight Oxygen-or-newer Karaf clusters on Linux control hosts with JVM heaps sized per Cisco-validated controllers when co-deployed; ONOS Hummingbird-or-later clusters with partitioned Raft-backed stores plus bonded uplinks through Linux leaf attachments.
